Solomon warns all young men, as if they were his children, to avoid physical desires. Some understand this “adulterous woman” to mean idolatry or false teaching, which leads people astray in their beliefs and behavior; but the main point is to warn against sins that violate the seventh commandment. These have often been, and still are, Satan’s way of drawing people from worshiping God into false religion. Consider how severe the consequences are; how bitter the results! It hurts no matter what. It leads to hell's torment. This sin directly leads to the destruction of body and soul. We must carefully avoid anything that could lead us toward it. Those who want to be protected from harm must stay away from situations where they might be tempted. If we put ourselves in temptation, we mock God when we pray, “Lead us not into temptation.” How many problems come with this sin! It damages your reputation; it wastes time; it ruins your finances; it harms your health; it will fill your mind with horror. Even if you are happy now, sooner or later it will bring sorrow. A sinner who realizes their mistake blames themselves and makes no excuses for their foolishness. Repeatedly committing sins strengthens the habit. A miracle of mercy might prevent the terrible consequences of such sins, but this is rare; most people die as they have lived. What can describe the situation of a person who has destroyed themself eternally, enduring the regret of their conscience?

Lawful marriage is a way God has established to avoid these destructive vices. But we are not properly united unless we pay attention to God’s word, seeking his guidance and blessing, and acting with affection. Always remember that even if secret sins escape the notice of other people, a person's ways are before the eyes of the Lord, who not only sees but considers all their actions. Those who are so foolish as to choose the path of sin are justly abandoned by God to themselves, to continue on the way to destruction.
